children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: An employee who regularly commutes from the employee’s place of residence in the continental United States to an official duty station in Canada or Mexico shall receive a border equalization pay adjustment equal to the amount of comparability payments under that the employee would receive if the employee were assigned to an official duty station within the United States locality pay area closest to the employee’s official duty station.section 5304 of title 5
is an “employee” as defined under ; andsection 2105 of title 5

is employed by the Department of State, the United States Agency for International Development, or the International Joint Commission of the United States and Canada (established under Article VII of the treaty signed ) (), except that the term shall not include members of the Service (as specified in ).January 11, 190936 Stat. 2448section 3903 of this title

children.heading: Treatment as basic pay
children.label: c Treatment as basic pay
children.text: An equalization pay adjustment paid under this section shall be considered to be part of basic pay for the same purposes for which comparability payments are considered to be part of basic pay under .section 5304 of title 5

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: d Regulations
children.text: The heads of the agencies referred to in subsection (b)(2) may prescribe regulations to carry out this section.
